transcript:
[00:00:00.00] Yeah, it's been about three years since I got this chair stuck on my body. Everything was going fine.
[00:00:06.00] I was just in a unregular outing, walking around the neighborhood, just doing whatever.
[00:00:13.00] There's a really handsome dog in the yard where I am on the street.
[00:00:19.00] And this dog says, with and this dog says, bark, I'm thinking, hey, a new friend.
[00:00:26.00] I go over to pet the dog. The dogs ears are very fluffy. The dog's mouth is not too smelly.
[00:00:33.00] After I finish with the dog, I walked some more, came across a manhole. Guess what's in the manhole?
[00:00:41.00] Never thought I would see this in a sewer. A big bus. In the sewer. So I'm thinking, hey, a new friend.
[00:00:54.00] I go see who's in the bus. I climbed down the manhole. As I'm going down the stairs, get bit by a black widow.
[00:01:02.00] I fall down the stairs, catapulting to my, what will be my death as I thought it was.
[00:01:11.00] I remember seeing everyone I had loved. I fell. I hit the ground. Splash splash. Thank you, man.
[00:01:21.00] I'm walking around in the dark. I hear rats. I hear bats. I hear spooky things. It's almost Halloween.
[00:01:44.00] I'm thinking about all the ones who I have loved. All of a sudden, I noticed a light. I turn on the light. Guess who's there? No one.
[00:01:54.00] But now I have a light. All of a sudden, two, four-legged beach chowls, like little cheetahs in the night, run towards me. They're counts.
[00:02:06.00] I get tackled. I get knocked over. As I'm holding onto one of these cheetah towels back rear. It flames us off for waterfall.
[00:02:18.00] I don't know how fast I'm falling. Somewhere close to a really great baseball pitchers throw pitch speed. Miles per hour. Somewhere. We fall. I fall. I get stuck in this chair.
[00:02:34.00] I'm going to need someone's backyard. Turns out that someone is a friend of my aunt's stepson. Just a side note. It's been about three years. Doctors, who do you need them?
[00:02:47.00] I'm getting used to it. I've tried every which way. I've tried. Glover. I've tried. Whatever you can try.
[00:02:55.00] Three years ago, when those cheetah beach chowls came to end my life, I knew that my life would never be the same ever again.
[00:03:05.00] If you were a president, would you, under the circumstances, find out a way to get this chair off my body?
